# GitHub repo page setup

Paste these into the repo's **About** panel (the gear icon, top-right of the
repo home page).

## About (description)

> A config-driven, self-managed **Canadian** retirement planning toolkit:
> RRSP/TFSA/RRIF/LIRA/FHSA/RESP modelling, CPP + OAS with claim-age logic, the OAS
> clawback and RRSP-meltdown strategy, a Monte Carlo engine, an HTML dashboard, and
> an employer-stock health monitor. Province-agnostic with an Ontario demo.
> Educational use only — not financial advice.

(Shorter, for the 350-char field:)

> Self-managed Canadian retirement planning toolkit — RRSP/TFSA/RRIF/FHSA/RESP,
> CPP+OAS, OAS clawback, RRSP-meltdown, Monte Carlo, dashboard, employer-stock
> monitor. Config-driven, runnable demo. Educational only; not advice.

retirement-planning

## Recommended repo settings
- **Website**: leave blank, or point to the rendered demo dashboard if you host one.
- **Include in the home page**: Releases off, Packages off (none here).
- Add a **CI badge** to the README (already included) so visitors see the demo
  passes on every push: `![CI](https://github.com/616fun/retirement-planning-toolkit-canada/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)`
- Consider enabling **Discussions** if you want the college / colleagues to ask
  setup questions.
- Link back to the US edition in the About → it's the sibling project.
